title: EDTA disodium salt dihydrate
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"3g13t","text":"For use in DNase I Treatment protocol for DNase inactivation.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":11,"length":17}],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"006E8E64FE194E34B4C2519A390B82BF","url":"https://www.protocols.io/view/DNase-I-Treatment-c3myk5"}}}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"4ii99","text":"Note: EDTA tetrasodium salt dihydrate is somewhat more soluble and can be made more easily to 1.5M. FW is slightly more so weigh out 3.80 g and follow directions above.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Weigh out 3.72 g EDTA and place in 5 ml molecular biology grade water.
Add a few NaOH pellets to get to pH 9 and dissolve EDTA (can warm to 45°C with stirring to aid in dissolution).
Measure volume and determine molarity: 1M = 3.72g/10 ml, therefore, measured volume/10 ml = final molarity.
creator: Bonnie Poulos
